claims.statement: The tool's canonical repository is github.com/nidor1998/s7cmd, published on crates.io as s7cmd by author nidor1998.
claims.qualification: The inherited row was not independently confirmed; groundwork cloned the repository and matched the crates.io publication.

claims.statement: s7cmd is written in Rust, edition 2024, rust-version 1.91.1.
claims.qualification: The inherited row presumed Rust; the pinned manifest confirms the edition and minimum toolchain.

claims.statement: s7cmd is licensed Apache-2.0.
claims.qualification: The inherited license row was unconfirmed; the pinned LICENSE resolves it.

claims.statement: The tested s7cmd version is v1.5.0 (commit d589df7), the latest release.
claims.qualification: The inherited version was unknown. The build receipt records the in-image string s7cmd 1.5.0; the commit and latest-release facet are source- and documentation-established, not receipt-recorded (the --version git-hash field was blank).

claims.statement: s7cmd's ls subcommand builds s3ls_rs::ListingPipeline directly from the s3ls-rs crate pinned at exactly =1.0.3; it is a crate dependency, not a separate reimplementation.
claims.qualification: The inherited notes speculated a possibly redundant or separately reimplemented listing engine; the pinned manifest and wrapper source establish a direct crate dependency.

claims.statement: The pinned s3ls-rs listing-engine crate is v1.0.3 (commit bf42067).
claims.qualification: The inherited s3ls-rs review was at v1.0.1; s7cmd depends on =1.0.3, and anchors were re-checked at that tag.

claims.statement: s7cmd is an umbrella CLI composing four of its author's Rust crates (s3sync, s3util-rs, s3rm-rs, and s3ls-rs), exposing ls, cp, mv, rm, sync, and clean plus bucket administration; only ls is in study scope.
claims.qualification: s3sync is the project's one human-written reference implementation; the other capabilities are out of this study's listing scope.

claims.statement: The author now points users to s7cmd over standalone s3ls-rs, asking that new issues be filed in the s7cmd repository.
claims.qualification: This is an upstream documentation statement in the s3ls-rs README.

claims.statement: s7cmd is the umbrella successor to the earlier standalone tools, while s3ls-rs is still released as a standalone crate.
claims.qualification: This resolves the inherited open question of successor versus separate project.

claims.statement: Standalone s3ls-rs remains maintained and released, with v1.0.3 tagged and not frozen.
claims.qualification: The standalone crate belongs to the archived s3ls-rs dossier; its continued release is documentation-supported.

claims.statement: Because s7cmd's ls compiles the same s3ls-rs crate version, the listing engine, its defaults, and its output formatters are identical to standalone s3ls by construction.
claims.qualification: This is the source-established engine-transfer part of the inherited open question; it does not extend to the CLI surface or to unmeasured full runtime equivalence.

claims.statement: s7cmd's ls CLI surface matches the s3ls flag set except that s7cmd deliberately hides each subcommand's inherited --auto-complete-shell in favor of one top-level flag; the committed ls --help receipt lacks it while standalone s3ls exposes it.
claims.qualification: This contradicts the inherited byte-identical / no-divergence wording flagged as an overclaim in review; the divergence is a CLI-surface difference, not an engine difference.

claims.statement: Full runtime equivalence between s7cmd ls and standalone s3ls beyond the shared crate and the two known divergences is inferred from the dependency pin, not established by a side-by-side comparative run.
claims.qualification: Standalone s3ls was not run side-by-side; equivalence beyond the shared crate is an inference from the dependency pin, not a measured result.

evidence.reason: Standalone s3ls was not run side-by-side to confirm behavioral identity.

claims.statement: s7cmd's ls_bin wrapper drops upstream s3ls-rs's load_config_exit_if_err helper (which called std::process::exit), so a bad Ls config inside a batch-run script does not kill the whole batch.
claims.qualification: This is the second of the two known CLI/process divergences from a stock s3ls-rs consumer.

claims.statement: The committed _build receipt records a binary that self-reports s7cmd 1.5.0 for the aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu target, with its full ls --help option surface captured.
claims.qualification: The inherited testability row was unknown. The receipt records only the resulting binary's self-report and help output; that this binary was built from s7cmd's own Dockerfile at the pinned SHA (arm64, native) is recorded in research/report.md section 7, not in a committed build log. No upstream image channel was confirmed (Docker Hub 404; GHCR unenumerable) per claim no-container-image-found-check-incomplete.

claims.statement: All twelve exercised s7cmd ls smoke mode/scope runs against noaa-normals-pds exited zero and the verifier returned PASS.
claims.qualification: PASS means normalized output matched the manifest for each exercised scope and is recorded in each run's verify.md; the exit-zero facet is recorded in each run's receipt.md. These are anonymous single-run groundwork facts, not benchmark results; the full mode-and-tunable inventory is claim modes-and-tunables-inventoried.

claims.statement: The full s7cmd ls surface was inventoried, resolving the inherited empty modes/tunables table: four output formatters (aligned, TSV, one-line, NDJSON); the recursive, shallow-delimiter, all-versions, max-depth, and bucket-listing modes; and the concurrency, fan-out-depth, queue-size, sort-threshold, max-keys, rate-limit, retry, and timeout tunables. The twelve smoke runs exercised the mode surface, while the tunable sweeps are deferred to the benchmark phase.
claims.qualification: The inherited modes/tunables table was empty; this inventories the surface from the committed --help capture and the pinned engine config without sweeping the tunables, which is benchmark-phase work.

claims.statement: Per the owner's 2026-07-17 decision, s7cmd is the s3ls-rs family's sole benchmark representative; standalone s3ls-rs is not separately benchmarked and s7cmd's results generalize to the crate, subject to the CLI-surface divergences that do not touch the listing engine.
claims.qualification: This is the study owner's 2026-07-17 scope decision, recorded in research/tool-page.md; the generalization it rests on is the =1.0.3 dependency pin established by claim ls-is-s3ls-rs-crate. The reconciliation record marks the inherited row unaddressed and routed to the orchestrator; the settled owner decision is recorded on the frozen tool page.

claims.statement: Upstream is active and self-described as functionally complete: created 2026-03-27, last pushed 2026-07-11, v1.5.0 tagged 2026-07-11, 221 commits, zero open issues and PRs, eight stars, zero forks, not archived, maintained with monthly dependency bumps.
claims.qualification: These are third-party GitHub API and README figures captured 2026-07-17.

claims.statement: The project states that all s7cmd, s3ls-rs, s3util-rs, and s3rm-rs source, tests, and docs are AI-generated (Claude Code) under human review with a stated 96%-plus test-coverage policy, with s3sync alone the human-written reference.
claims.qualification: Flagged as unusual provenance worth the study owner's attention given this study's own do-not-trust-unverified-prose premise, though the source read during groundwork was coherent and well-tested.

claims.statement: No upstream container-image channel was found (Docker Hub returned 404), but the check is incomplete: GitHub Packages and GHCR could not be enumerated, so this is not a confirmed universal negative; the smoke image was built from s7cmd's own Dockerfile instead.
claims.qualification: This softens the inherited categorical no-image-published-anywhere wording: GHCR returned 403 for a missing read:packages scope and an anonymous GHCR token was denied, so that channel remains unverified. research/report.md records the Docker Hub 404 and the GHCR 403/denied-token probes.

claims.statement: The engine's api_calls counter is an AtomicU64 incremented once immediately before each fetch_page call in both the sequential and parallel paths, so it counts logical page-fetch operations, not wire-level S3 HTTP requests; because the client uses the AWS SDK standard retry mode (up to ten attempts by default), one counted page fetch can correspond to more than one chargeable HTTP request.
claims.qualification: This corrects an inherited framing of api_calls as a request count; establishing an exact wire-level request count needs SDK tracing or an external counter and is handed to the study's Phase 2 replay instrument.

claims.statement: The full-bucket recursive smoke run recorded 204 counted page fetches against an approximately 149-page sequential floor (148,917 keys divided by 1000 max_keys).
claims.qualification: This is a page-fetch count from one anonymous smoke run, not a wire-level request count or a benchmark result. The excess over the sequential floor reflects the engine's delimiter-discovery pages mixing objects and prefixes on top of the leaf drain, the source-established shape (claim flat-bucket-collapses-to-sequential; anchor cited below), not a receipt-recorded cause.

claims.statement: Counted page fetches vary with prefix scope across individual smoke runs: a depth-3 leaf prefix of 9,839 keys recorded 10 page fetches, a shallower prefix of 15,625 keys recorded 19, and the shallow and --max-depth 1 root listings each recorded exactly one API call.
claims.qualification: These are page-fetch counts from individual anonymous smoke runs, not benchmark measurements. That the counts track leaf-versus-branch prefix shape is the source-established drain behavior (claim flat-bucket-collapses-to-sequential), not a measured cross-run result.

claims.statement: The -r --all-versions mode switches the API to ListObjectVersions and adds VersionId and possible delete-marker rows, but the IsLatest column is not part of the default output: it requires the separate --show-is-latest flag, and the committed all-versions smoke run invoked --all-versions --tsv without --show-is-latest.
claims.qualification: This corrects an inherited overclaim about the all-versions output contract; --show-is-latest itself requires all_versions and the aligned formatter emits IS_LATEST only when that flag and a version id are present. The source anchors back the output contract; the run receipt records the invocation. The run's full TSV stdout (VersionId column, no IsLatest) is an external release-asset payload, not a committed artifact.

claims.statement: Versioned-bucket fidelity for all-versions mode is deferred, not settled: the smoke bucket has only single null-version objects, so multi-version collapse, delete-marker rows, and IsLatest semantics were never exercised, and the mode passed only because there was nothing to collapse.
claims.qualification: EDGE_BUCKET=none for this pass; a version-aware manifest and a versioned reference bucket are prerequisites, handed to the benchmark phase.

evidence.reason: The smoke bucket has only single null-version objects and EDGE_BUCKET=none, so genuine multi-version collapse, delete markers, and IsLatest were never exercised.

claims.statement: The engine chooses between a bucket-listing path and an object-listing pipeline based on whether a target bucket is present.
claims.qualification: The split is made in the s7cmd wrapper before dispatching to the s3ls-rs pipeline.

claims.statement: The engine connects its async stages with bounded tokio::mpsc channels whose object-listing-queue-size defaults to 200000.
claims.qualification: The bound provides backpressure between the lister, aggregator, and display stages.

claims.statement: The pipeline awaits its stages terminal-first (display then aggregator then lister), with a writer error flipping the cancellation token and error precedence running display over aggregator over lister.
claims.qualification: Cancellation is driven by the awaiting code and the lister rather than by the error-precedence ordering itself.

claims.statement: Parallel listing is used only when max_parallel_listings is greater than one, the delimiter is None (recursive mode), and the bucket is not Express One Zone unless the opt-in flag is set.
claims.qualification: Non-recursive listings always set a slash delimiter and run sequentially regardless of the concurrency flag.

claims.statement: The sequential listing path checks cancellation, applies the rate limit, bumps the page counter, fetches a page, sends results, and loops while the response is truncated.
claims.qualification: This is the single-level paginated path used for non-recursive and forced-sequential listings.

claims.statement: The parallel path uses a tokio JoinSet bounded by a semaphore sized to max_parallel_listings, paginates each prefix with a slash delimiter, drops the parent permit before spawning child tasks, and switches to sequential drain once recursion passes the fan-out depth.
claims.qualification: Dropping the parent permit before spawning children is the deadlock-avoidance rule; the parent must not hold a worker permit while blocked on children that also need permits. The semaphore is sized to max_parallel_listings at :789 and constructed at :825.

claims.statement: Two distinct depth concepts exist: the internal fan-out depth max_parallel_listing_max_depth (default 2) versus the user-facing --max-depth content limit, which synthesizes a CommonPrefix entry at the boundary instead of recursing further.
claims.qualification: The two are easy to conflate but govern fan-out versus output depth respectively.

claims.statement: At the bucket root, --max-depth 1 makes exactly one API call and emits PRE rows at the boundary without recursing.
claims.qualification: This settles the boundary-synthesis path against the registered smoke bucket in a single anonymous run; the receipt records api_calls=1 and the stdout records the boundary PRE rows.

claims.statement: Express One Zone buckets are detected via a --x-s3 name suffix, and parallel listing on them is rejected by default and requires an explicit opt-in flag.
claims.qualification: This is source-confirmed but not runtime-tested; no Express One Zone bucket was available.

claims.statement: The ObjectLister drops the fetcher channel's receive side before joining the storage task, a deliberate deadlock-avoidance pattern pinned by a named regression test.
claims.qualification: The named test is lister_does_not_deadlock_when_cancelled_with_full_queue.

claims.statement: The aggregator forwards each entry immediately in --no-sort streaming mode or buffers every entry and sorts before emitting in the default mode.
claims.qualification: The pipeline only wires the aggregator config through; the aggregator itself implements streaming versus buffering.

claims.statement: Default-mode sorting uses rayon par_sort_by only past --parallel-sort-threshold (default 1,000,000); below the threshold, the stdlib stable sort_by runs instead.
claims.qualification: The threshold governs the stdlib-versus-rayon switch inside the aggregator.

claims.statement: The smoke run logged parallel_sort_threshold=1000000 with entry counts far below it, so the parallel-sort switch was never exercised.
claims.qualification: The 2,549-key run's stderr records the threshold and entry count directly; that staying below the threshold means the stdlib sort ran rather than rayon is the source-established branch (claim sort-uses-rayon-past-threshold), not a receipt-recorded fact, so this is supported rather than confirmed.

claims.statement: The DisplayWriter selects one of four formatters (aligned, TSV, one-line, or JSON) once and writes to a buffered stdout sink.
claims.qualification: Formatter selection happens once at pipeline construction.

claims.statement: The text formatters escape control characters below 0x20 and 0x7f to hex, use a borrowed-Cow fast path when no escaping is needed, allow opt-out via --raw-output, and exempt JSON output, which uses serde.
claims.qualification: The smoke bucket's keys are plain ASCII, so the escaping path was not stressed at runtime. The JSON-exemption facet is anchored in display/json.rs, which formats via serde rather than the text escaper.

claims.statement: s7cmd ls with no target calls ListBuckets; anonymously S3 returns a 307 redirect and the tool exits 1, so bucket listing is blocked rather than silently skipped.
claims.qualification: This is a capability probe under CREDS=none: the receipt records exit 1, the stderr records the 307 redirect and that no-sign-request disabled credential loading and signing, and the wrapper's bucket-versus-object split selects ListBuckets when no target is given.

claims.statement: The bucket-listing mode's max_buckets and use_max_buckets internals were not source-read.
claims.qualification: The anonymous probe established only that listing buckets is blocked; the internal parameters were not audited.

evidence.reason: The bucket-listing max_buckets and use_max_buckets internals were not source-read.

claims.statement: The bucket-listing mode issues a single async list_buckets call and exposes a max_buckets default of 1000, a --bucket-name-prefix filter, and a use_max_buckets flag for custom endpoints.
claims.qualification: This conserves the inherited dossier's bucket-listing-mode description at its base documentation strength; s7cmd groundwork did not independently source-read the max_buckets and use_max_buckets internals, which stays a separate observation (claim bucket-listing-internals-unread).

claims.statement: The engine guards pagination with two deliberate bails rather than looping forever: a truncated page with no continuation token, or the same continuation token returned twice in a row.
claims.qualification: These guards are source-confirmed but were not runtime-triggered against well-behaved real S3.

claims.statement: Whether the stuck-continuation-token bail yields an incomplete listing against a misbehaving endpoint is untested.
claims.qualification: Real S3 did not produce a stuck token, so the bail path was never runtime-triggered.

evidence.reason: The bail path was not runtime-triggered; real S3 was well-behaved.

claims.statement: The engine implements no self-retry loop; it configures the AWS SDK standard retry mode with exponential backoff and jitter, defaulting to ten attempts and a 100 ms initial backoff, and leaves the SDK to own retries.
claims.qualification: Stalled-stream protection is on by default and no operation, connect, or read timeout is set unless the user passes one.

claims.statement: The engine's tunable defaults (max_parallel_listings 64, fan-out depth 2, queue size 200000, sort threshold 1,000,000, max_keys 1000, retry attempts 10, backoff 100 ms, and a rate-limit floor and refill of one-tenth) are declared and consumed as described and remain current at v1.0.3.
claims.qualification: The constants are declared in the argument config and consumed in semaphore sizing and limiter construction.

claims.statement: The default sorted mode buffers every entry into a vector before sorting or emitting anything, while --no-sort streams at near-constant memory.
claims.qualification: This is the source-established memory behavior, distinct from any scale measurement.

claims.statement: The approximately linear RSS growth of the buffering mode and the documented roughly ninefold sorted-versus-nosort gap are not settled at smoke scale.
claims.qualification: The smoke peaks are consistent with the linear model but do not measure it; the gap appears only near the 1,000,000-key threshold and needs a scale campaign.

evidence.reason: No object-count scaling campaign was run; smoke peaks do not establish scaling or the sorted-versus-nosort gap.

claims.statement: The full-bucket recursive smoke run sampled a 120.8 MB peak RSS at 148,917 keys.
claims.qualification: This is a main-process high-water mark from one anonymous smoke run, consistent with the linear model but not itself a measurement of it at scale, and not a benchmark result.

claims.statement: Whether whole-bucket listing of a billion-object bucket is the worst regime is unaddressed.
claims.qualification: This is a scale-only hypothesis that is not settleable at smoke.

evidence.reason: Scale-only hypothesis; not settleable at smoke.

claims.statement: s7cmd ls is a one-shot CLI with no persisted state; SIGINT cancels the pipeline via a cancellation token and exits 0, and no resume or checkpoint of any kind exists.
claims.qualification: This confirms the inherited no-crash-resume hypothesis from the wrapper source and the upstream Non-Goals.

claims.statement: A bucket with no slash hierarchy discovers zero sub-prefixes in the delimiter-discovery loop and the whole listing collapses to sequential pagination; the default fan-out depth is 2.
claims.qualification: The keyspace is divided by delimiter-based common-prefix recursion to a fixed depth, then a flat sequential drain of each leaf, not by bisection or cut-points.

claims.statement: The reduced speedup a flat or shallow-layout bucket gets from fan-out is unmeasured at smoke scale.
claims.qualification: The registered smoke bucket is hierarchical; the flat-keyspace regression is handed to the benchmark phase.

evidence.reason: The smoke bucket is hierarchical; no flat-bucket run was performed.

claims.statement: The --rate-limit-api control has a floor of 10 requests per second enforced by a CLI validator and refills at one-tenth of the configured value.
claims.qualification: The floor is a CLI validator and the refill fraction is set at limiter construction.

claims.statement: The latency step at the 1,000,000-key sort threshold, where the stdlib sort gives way to rayon, is unmeasured.
claims.qualification: Smoke entry counts stayed far below the threshold, so the runtime step in the latency curve was never observed.

evidence.reason: Smoke entry counts stayed far below the 1,000,000-key threshold; the latency step was not measured.

claims.statement: The inherited v1.0.1 line anchors were re-checked at v1.0.3; most land within a few lines, and drifted anchors such as the semaphore-size and max-depth-boundary sites were corrected.
claims.qualification: This is an anchor-location correction carried into mechanism.md and research/report.md, not a behavior change.

claims.statement: The engine's declared dependencies include tokio, aws-sdk-s3, rayon, leaky-bucket, fancy-regex, and zeroize among others.
claims.qualification: The dependency set is read from the s3ls-rs manifest at v1.0.3.

claims.statement: Whether access keys are zeroized on drop via the zeroize dependency was not source-verified.
claims.qualification: zeroize appears in the dependency inventory, but the on-drop behavior of access keys was not read.

evidence.reason: The zeroize-on-drop behavior of access keys was not source-verified.
